content.problemStatement:
Given a string, count number of vowels in it and print the count.

**Note:** lowercase vowels are: `a`, `e`, `i`, `o` and `u`.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN - the length of string
The second line of input will contain a string of length n consists of only lowercase english letters.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the number of vowels in given string.
constraints: The string contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string; the second line contains a string of length N consisting only of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u) present in the given string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The string "codechef" contains three vowels: 'o' and two 'e's.
The string "hello" contains two vowels: 'e' and 'o'.

solution.approach:
For each test case we simply scan the given string character by character and increase a counter whenever the character is one of the five lowercase vowels. This linear scan yields the exact count of vowels.
The algorithm is straightforward: read N (not strictly needed for counting but part of the input format), read the string, iterate over its characters, check membership in the set {a,e,i,o,u}, and output the counter.
Because each character is examined once, the solution runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total length of all strings) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input string.
